event_name: Bách Việt groups faced territorial pressure and migration during the Spring and Autumn–Warring States period
description_short: From about the 8th to 3rd centuries BCE, Việt groups south of the Yangtze faced pressure from Chu and Qin and migration from northern Chinese populations.
description_long: The Vietnamese narrative source describes territorial loss, assimilation, and fragmentation among different Việt groups during the Spring and Autumn–Warring States period.

description_long: The source frames this as a broad transformation from loose Việt groups into separate polities from the 8th century BCE onward.
